title: Living for God in Hostile Times
subtitle: Arming with Christ mindset, loving earnestly, and sharing in Christ sufferings with joy

heb.paragraph: Arm yourselves with the same way of thinking - Christ suffered in the flesh; adopt his mindset. Suffering may mean cessation of sin.
The litany of pagan vices: sensuality, passions, drunkenness, orgies, drinking parties, lawless idolatry. The former life of the readers.
They malign you for not joining them - the same verb used of reviling God. Refusal to participate provokes slander.

panels.ctx: Peter returns to the theme of suffering with a new angle: arm yourselves with Christ mindset. Whoever has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin - not perfectionism but decisive break with the old life. The time past is sufficient for doing what the Gentiles want: sensuality, passions, drunkenness, orgies, drinking parties, lawless idolatry. Former companions are surprised and malign believers for not joining in. But they will give account to him who judges the living and the dead. Even the dead had the gospel preached to them so that they might live in the spirit.

cross.note: How can we who died to sin still live in it?
Let us walk properly, not in orgies and drunkenness.
You must no longer walk as the Gentiles do, in futility.
He is the one appointed by God to be judge of the living and the dead.

notes.note: Arm yourselves with Christ way of thinking. Whoever has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin - to live for the will of God.
The time past suffices for Gentile behavior: sensuality, passions, drunkenness, orgies. They malign you for not joining them now.
They will give account to him who judges living and dead. The gospel was preached even to the dead, that they might live in the spirit.
Calvin takes ceased from sin as decisive break, not sinless perfection. The flesh suffers when denied its desires; this suffering kills sin.
The dead who heard the gospel are those who heard and believed during their lifetime, not those evangelized postmortem.
The phrase arm yourselves uses a military metaphor - putting on armor for battle. The battle is against the old life.
The dead are most likely believers who heard the gospel while alive and have since died. Peter assures readers that death does not cancel salvation.
Schreiner emphasizes the already/not-yet tension: believers have broken with sin (past tense) yet must continue to resist (ongoing).
Jobes notes that the readers former friends are hostile to their conversion. Social pressure was intense; Peter encourages perseverance.

sections.header: Verses 7-11 - "The End Is Near: Love and Serve"

heb.gloss: to be of sound mind, self-controlled
heb.paragraph: Be self-controlled and sober-minded for the sake of your prayers - eschatological urgency demands spiritual discipline.
Above all, keep loving one another earnestly, since love covers a multitude of sins - the priority of community love.
Each has received a gift; use it to serve one another as good stewards of God varied grace. Gifts are for service, not display.

panels.ctx: The end of all things is at hand - eschatological urgency motivates ethics. Be self-controlled and sober for prayer. Above all, love earnestly - love covers a multitude of sins (Proverbs 10:12). Show hospitality without grumbling. Each has a gift; use it to serve as stewards of God varied grace. Speakers should speak as oracles of God; servers should serve by God strength - so that God may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom belong glory and dominion forever.

cross.note: The coming of the Lord is at hand.
Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all offenses.
Having gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, let us use them.
Whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.

notes.note: The end is at hand. Be self-controlled and sober-minded for prayer. Above all, love earnestly - love covers a multitude of sins.
Show hospitality without grumbling. Use your gift to serve. Speak as oracles of God; serve by God strength. That God may be glorified.
Calvin notes that love covering sins means forgiving offenses, not condoning them. Love does not broadcast faults but absorbs them.
Gifts are deposits, not possessions. Stewardship implies accountability. Every gift is for service, and all service glorifies God.
The phrase love covers sins alludes to Proverbs 10:12. Love forgives and forbears rather than exposing and publicizing.
The doxology to whom belong glory and dominion could refer to God or Christ. Context suggests Christ is the antecedent.
Schreiner sees this as a mini-exhortation on community life: love, hospitality, gift-stewardship - all under eschatological urgency.
Jobes notes that hospitality was essential for traveling Christians and missionaries. Without hotels, believers depended on one another.

heb.paragraph: Do not be surprised at the fiery trial - trials are to be expected, not exceptions. The furnace tests gold; suffering tests faith.
Rejoice insofar as you share Christ sufferings - participation in Christ includes participation in his suffering. Glory follows.
Let none suffer as a murderer, thief, evildoer, or meddler - suffering must be for righteousness, not wrongdoing.

panels.ctx: Beloved, do not be surprised by the fiery ordeal - it tests you. Rejoice as you share Christ sufferings, so that you may rejoice at his glory revelation. If insulted for Christ name, you are blessed - the Spirit of glory rests on you. Do not suffer as a murderer or thief or evildoer or meddler. But if as a Christian, do not be ashamed; glorify God in that name. Judgment begins with God household - if it starts with us, what will be the outcome for the disobedient? If the righteous is scarcely saved, what of the ungodly? Those who suffer according to God will should entrust their souls to a faithful Creator while doing good.

cross.note: They rejoiced that they were counted worthy to suffer dishonor for the name.
If children, then heirs - heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ, provided we suffer with him.
Begin at my sanctuary - judgment starting with God house.
If the righteous is repaid on earth, how much more the wicked and the sinner!

notes.note: Do not be surprised at the fiery trial. Rejoice insofar as you share Christ sufferings, that you may rejoice at his glory revelation.
If insulted for Christ name, you are blessed. Do not suffer as evildoer or meddler. If as a Christian, glorify God in that name.
Judgment begins with God household. If the righteous is scarcely saved, what of the ungodly? Entrust your souls to a faithful Creator.
Calvin sees the fiery ordeal as refining, not destroying. The Spirit of glory resting on sufferers is present comfort, not just future hope.
Judgment begins with God house - not punitive but purifying. If believers face discipline, unbelievers face destruction.
The term allotriepiskopos (meddler) is rare - possibly one who interferes in others affairs or encroaches on others rights.
The term Christianos (Christian) appears only three times in the NT (here, Acts 11:26, 26:28). Originally a derisive label, Peter reclaims it.
Schreiner emphasizes the distinction between suffering for Christ (blessed) and suffering for sin (deserved). Only the former participates in Christ sufferings.
Jobes notes that the name Christian was a social identifier that provoked hostility. To bear it publicly required courage.

rows.label: vv. 1-6
rows.text: Arm yourselves; break with the past

lit.note: Chapter 4 intensifies the suffering theme. Believers must break with the past (vv. 1-6), serve each other (vv. 7-11), and rejoice in persecution (vv. 12-19).

themes.note: Judgment is prominent: the end is near; judgment begins with God house. Holiness is strong in the break with pagan lifestyle. Justice appears in the contrast between suffering for righteousness and suffering for sin.
